title: Persistent net losses
description: Company reports net losses for multiple consecutive years with no clear path to profitability. Burn rate may exceed revenue growth, increasing dilution or bankruptcy risk.

title: Negative EBITDA
description: Negative EBITDA indicates the core business cannot cover operating expenses before depreciation and interest. For growth-stage companies this is expected, but concerning if it persists beyond Year 5.

title: Revenue growth masking margin compression
description: Top-line growth is strong but gross margins are declining year-over-year. Company may be buying growth through discounts, incentives, or unsustainable pricing — not building durable economics.

title: High cash burn relative to reserves
description: Operating cash outflows are large relative to cash on hand, giving the company less than 18 months of runway. Risk of needing emergency capital raise at unfavorable terms.

title: Debt covenant risk
description: Company carries debt with financial covenants (leverage ratios, EBITDA minimums). If operating performance deteriorates, covenants may be breached, triggering accelerated repayment.

title: Refinancing wall approaching
description: Significant debt maturities are concentrated in the next 12-24 months. In a high-interest-rate environment, refinancing may be significantly more expensive or unavailable.

title: Slowing user or customer growth
description: Active user or customer counts are growing slower than revenue, indicating the company is extracting more from existing users rather than expanding. Saturation risk in core markets.

title: Incentive dependency
description: Revenue or user growth is heavily dependent on subsidies, cashback, or promotional incentives. Reducing incentives to improve margins may trigger user churn or GMV decline.

title: Geographic concentration risk
description: Majority of revenue comes from one or two markets. Regulatory changes, currency devaluation, or competitive entry in those markets would have outsized impact on total performance.

title: Multi-jurisdiction regulatory exposure
description: Company operates across multiple countries with varying fintech, data privacy, and labor regulations. A single regulatory action in a key market can materially impair operations.

title: Fintech or banking license risk
description: Company holds or is applying for digital banking or payments licenses. License revocation, denial, or onerous conditions could shut down a key revenue segment.

title: Data privacy and cybersecurity liability
description: Business model requires storing sensitive user financial or personal data across multiple jurisdictions. A breach or non-compliance could trigger large fines and reputational damage.

title: Intense competition from well-funded rivals
description: Market has two or more well-capitalized competitors pursuing the same user base. Price wars or incentive wars can quickly erode unit economics for all players.

title: Big tech platform risk
description: A large platform (Google, Meta, Amazon, Alibaba, Tencent) operates in or is expanding into the same space. Incumbents have cost of distribution and data advantages that are hard to match.

title: Dilution risk from future fundraising
description: Company will likely need additional capital before reaching profitability. Future equity raises will dilute existing shareholders, especially if done at a valuation discount.

title: Share-based compensation inflating losses
description: High stock-based compensation (SBC) as a percentage of revenue inflates operating losses. While non-cash, it represents real economic dilution and signals high talent retention costs.

title: Driver or merchant partner dependency
description: Business model relies on gig-economy workers or third-party merchants. Regulatory reclassification of contractors as employees would significantly increase labor costs.

title: Technology infrastructure reliability risk
description: Platform downtime or system failures directly translate to lost transactions and user trust. High dependence on third-party cloud providers introduces concentration risk.

title: Emerging market currency risk
description: Revenue generated in local currencies (SGD, MYR, PHP, IDR) while costs or debt may be USD-denominated. Unfavorable FX movements compress reported USD financials.

title: Interest rate sensitivity
description: Company holds significant floating-rate debt or cash-flow-negative operations funded by external capital. Rising interest rates increase financing costs and reduce equity value.

title: Dual-class share structure limiting minority rights
description: Founders or insiders hold super-voting shares, limiting minority shareholder ability to influence board decisions or executive compensation. Governance risk in adverse scenarios.

title: Related party transactions
description: Material transactions with entities controlled by founders, directors, or major shareholders. Creates potential for conflicts of interest and value extraction at minority expense.

title: Gross profit insufficient to cover fixed costs
description: Even at full scale, gross profit dollars may not cover fixed operating expenses (R&D, G&A, sales). Business model may be structurally unprofitable without significant scale gains.

title: Segment profitability divergence
description: One profitable segment subsidizes loss-making segments. If the profitable segment is mature, overall company profitability path depends on turning around structurally challenged businesses.

title: Negative operating cash flow despite accounting profit
description: Company reports GAAP profit but negative operating cash flow, often due to aggressive revenue recognition or working capital deterioration. Cash is more reliable than reported earnings.
